reforms.description: The Five-Year Plan for Green Growth includes approximately 600 projects with a total budget of 108.7 trillion won, aiming to enhance public R&D funding to address market failures in green innovation.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Increased public R&D funding is expected to stimulate private-sector innovation and address environmental challenges, contributing to sustainable economic growth.
reforms.status_evidence: The plan was launched with a defined budget and projects.
reforms.importance_rationale: This plan represents a significant structural change in Korea's approach to green innovation, with a large budget allocation aimed at transforming the economy towards sustainability.
reforms.source_quote: The Five-Year Plan (2009-13) contains about 600 projects and a total budget of 108.7 trillion won (10% of 2009 GDP).

reforms.description: The government introduced a cap-and-trade emissions trading scheme (ETS) to promote green innovation and achieve GHG emission reduction targets, set to be implemented in 2015.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: The ETS is designed to create economic incentives for firms to innovate and reduce emissions, fostering a transition to a low-carbon economy.
reforms.status_evidence: The government introduced legislation in 2011 to create a cap-and-trade ETS in 2015.
reforms.importance_rationale: The introduction of the ETS represents a major shift in environmental policy, aiming to integrate market mechanisms into Korea's approach to emissions reduction and innovation.
reforms.source_quote: The government introduced legislation in 2011 to create a cap-and-trade ETS in 2015 covering firms with annual emissions of more than 25 thousand tonnes.

reforms.description: Korea aims to enhance the contribution of higher education to innovation by promoting links with government and business research institutes and increasing the share of government R&D funding that is allocated competitively.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Strengthening ties between academia and industry is expected to enhance innovation and improve the effectiveness of R&D investments.
reforms.status_evidence: The government has outlined plans to enhance the contribution of higher education to innovation.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform is significant as it seeks to improve the interaction between higher education and research sectors, which is crucial for fostering innovation.
reforms.source_quote: Enhance the contribution of higher education to innovation by promoting links with government and business research institutes.

reforms.description: Korea plans to expand investment in early childhood education and care (ECEC) to achieve the objective of free education for children aged three to five and upgrade its quality.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Investing in early childhood education is expected to yield high returns by improving future educational outcomes and workforce readiness.
reforms.status_evidence: The government has announced plans to expand investment in ECEC.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform is significant as it addresses foundational education, which is critical for long-term economic growth and human capital development.
reforms.source_quote: Expand investment in ECEC to achieve the objective of free education for children aged three to five.

reforms.description: The government introduced 'Meister' schools to enhance vocational training, focusing on workplace training to facilitate the transition to the job market.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Improving vocational education increases the supply of skilled workers, which can enhance productivity and innovation in the labor market.
reforms.status_evidence: The government has launched several initiatives towards improving vocational training.
reforms.importance_rationale: The introduction of Meister schools represents a significant step towards improving vocational education, which is crucial for addressing labor market mismatches and enhancing human capital.
reforms.source_quote: To upgrade vocational training, the government introduced 'Meister' schools, in which workplace training plays a key role, thereby facilitating the transition to the job market.

reforms.description: The government is reducing public funds to universities ranking in the bottom 15% starting in 2012, aiming to improve the quality of tertiary education.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: By incentivizing universities to improve their quality, this reform aims to enhance the overall educational outcomes and contribute to a more skilled workforce.
reforms.status_evidence: The government is reducing public funds to universities ranking in the bottom 15% beginning in 2012.
reforms.importance_rationale: This funding reduction is a significant measure aimed at improving the quality of higher education, which is essential for fostering innovation and economic growth.
reforms.source_quote: The government is reducing public funds to universities ranking in the bottom 15% beginning in 2012.

reforms.description: The government introduced R&D tax credits for 11 knowledge-based service sectors, including health care, in 2012 to boost R&D in services.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: The tax credits incentivize private sector investment in R&D, particularly in underfunded service sectors, which can enhance innovation and productivity.
reforms.status_evidence: The government introduced R&D tax credits for knowledge-based service sectors in 2012.
reforms.importance_rationale: The introduction of R&D tax credits represents a significant policy shift aimed at enhancing R&D investment in services, which is crucial for economic diversification and growth.
reforms.source_quote: To boost R&D in services, the government introduced R&D tax credits for 11 knowledge-based service sectors, including health care, in 2012.

reforms.description: The government introduced the 'National Employment Strategy 2020' aimed at increasing the employment rate by making better use of vulnerable workers, including women, the elderly, and youth, through various supportive measures.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: By increasing the participation of underrepresented groups in the labor force, the strategy aims to enhance overall productivity and economic growth.
reforms.status_evidence: The strategy was reported as part of the government's efforts to boost employment.
reforms.importance_rationale: This strategy represents a significant structural change in labor market policy, targeting key demographic groups to enhance labor force participation and productivity.
reforms.source_quote: The government’s 2010 'National Employment Strategy 2020' aims at making greater use of 'vulnerable workers', such as women, the elderly and youth, to boost the employment rate.

reforms.description: The government introduced a law in 2010 to prohibit age discrimination in recruitment and employment, aiming to enhance the employment prospects of older workers.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: By prohibiting age discrimination, the reform aims to increase the participation of older workers in the labor market, thereby enhancing overall productivity and economic growth.
reforms.status_evidence: The law was enacted to prohibit age discrimination in recruitment and employment.
reforms.importance_rationale: This law represents a significant step towards improving labor market conditions for older workers, which is crucial given Korea's demographic challenges.
reforms.source_quote: The 2008 Age Discrimination Act prohibits hiring based on age.

reforms.description: The government announced the 'Second Basic Employment Promotion Plan (2012-16) for the Aged', which includes various initiatives to support the employment of older workers.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: The plan aims to enhance the employability of older workers, thereby increasing their participation in the labor force and contributing to economic growth.
reforms.status_evidence: The government announced the 'Second Basic Employment Promotion Plan (2012-16) for the Aged'.
reforms.importance_rationale: This plan includes multiple initiatives aimed at improving employment conditions for older workers, which is significant given the demographic challenges Korea faces.
reforms.source_quote: In December 2011, the government announced the 'Second Basic Employment Promotion Plan (2012-16) for the Aged'.

reforms.description: The government has increased support to colleges, particularly two-year institutions, by doubling the public share of their funding from 6% in 2005 to 12% in 2009, while also reducing state intervention to enhance their autonomy.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Increased funding and autonomy for colleges are expected to improve educational quality and responsiveness to labor market needs, thereby enhancing human capital.
reforms.status_evidence: The public share of funding for colleges was doubled.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform significantly enhances funding for tertiary education, which is crucial for developing skilled human capital in response to changing economic conditions.
reforms.source_quote: The public share of their funding was doubled from 6% in 2005 to 12% in 2009.

reforms.description: The government introduced a new quality management system for tertiary institutions, requiring self-evaluation and public disclosure of education and research activities to enhance accountability and quality.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: By promoting accountability and transparency, the reform is expected to drive improvements in educational quality and outcomes, thereby enhancing the skill level of the workforce.
reforms.status_evidence: Universities were required to conduct self-evaluation and publicly disclose results.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform introduces a significant shift towards accountability in higher education, which is essential for improving educational quality and aligning it with labor market needs.
reforms.source_quote: The government introduced a new quality management system for tertiary institutions in 2007.

reforms.description: The government established the 'Study Korea Project Plan' in 2008, aiming to attract 100,000 foreign students by 2012 through improved competitiveness of higher education.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Attracting foreign students is expected to enhance the quality of education and provide high-skilled human resources, contributing positively to the economy.
reforms.status_evidence: The government established the 'Study Korea Project Plan' in 2008.
reforms.importance_rationale: This initiative represents a significant effort to enhance the international profile of Korean higher education, which is crucial for attracting talent and improving educational outcomes.
reforms.source_quote: The government established the 'Study Korea Project Plan' in 2008, with a target of attracting 100 thousand foreign students by 2012.

reforms.description: The Korean government introduced the '577 Initiative' aiming to increase gross domestic expenditure on R&D (GERD) to 5% of GDP by 2012, positioning Korea among the top science and technology powers globally.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Increasing R&D funding is expected to enhance innovation capacity and productivity, contributing positively to economic growth.
reforms.status_evidence: The initiative was aimed at achieving a specific GERD target by 2012.
reforms.importance_rationale: This initiative represents a significant structural change in R&D funding policy, aiming to elevate Korea's global standing in science and technology.
reforms.source_quote: The '577 Initiative' targets an increase in GERD to 5% of GDP in 2012 to make Korea one of the 'seven major science and technology powers in the world'.

reforms.description: The government signed an agreement with five major business organizations to provide more job opportunities for high school graduates, particularly from vocational schools, addressing the mismatch in the labor market.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: By improving job opportunities for vocational graduates, the reform aims to enhance labor market efficiency and productivity.
reforms.status_evidence: The government signed an agreement to enhance job opportunities for vocational school graduates.
reforms.importance_rationale: This agreement represents a significant step towards aligning education with labor market needs, which is crucial for economic growth.
reforms.source_quote: In 2011, the government signed an agreement with five major business organisations that agreed to provide more job opportunities for high school graduates, especially from vocational schools.

reforms.description: The government is stimulating R&D investment in knowledge-based service industries by providing tax credits, aiming to enhance innovation and productivity in the sector.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: By incentivizing R&D in services, the policy aims to boost productivity and innovation, which can lead to economic growth.
reforms.status_evidence: The government is actively providing tax credits to stimulate R&D investment.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform represents a significant effort to enhance R&D in the service sector, which has historically lagged behind manufacturing.
reforms.source_quote: The government is trying to stimulate R&D investment by providing tax credits to knowledge-based service industries.

reforms.description: The government has been removing entry barriers and boosting competition in the service sector to enhance productivity and innovation.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: By fostering competition, the policy aims to improve service sector productivity, which can lead to overall economic growth.
reforms.status_evidence: The government has significantly reduced entry barriers in the service sector.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform is significant as it addresses structural issues in the service sector that hinder productivity and innovation.
reforms.source_quote: The government has been removing entry barriers and trying to boost competition in services.

reforms.description: Korea adopted a Green Growth Strategy in 2009, aiming to reduce greenhouse gas emissions by 30% by 2020 and promote innovation in green technology through a Five-Year Plan with significant public spending.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: The strategy aims to foster innovation in green technologies, which can lead to new economic growth engines and job creation while addressing climate change.
reforms.status_evidence: The Green Growth Strategy has been actively pursued since its announcement.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform represents a comprehensive approach to integrating environmental sustainability with economic growth, impacting multiple sectors and fostering innovation.
reforms.source_quote: Korea adopted an ambitious Green Growth Strategy in 2009, aiming at reducing emissions by 30% by 2020.

reforms.description: The government replaced the Feed-In-Tariff (FIT) system with a Renewable Portfolio Standard (RPS) in January 2012, obliging electricity companies to produce or buy a specified share of their electricity from renewable sources, with targets increasing from 2% in 2012 to 10% by 2022.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: The RPS is designed to promote competition in the renewable sector and stimulate innovation by creating a market for renewable energy certificates, thereby encouraging investment in green technologies.
reforms.status_evidence: The RPS was introduced in January 2012, replacing the previous FIT system.
reforms.importance_rationale: The shift from FIT to RPS represents a significant structural change in Korea's renewable energy policy, aimed at enhancing market dynamics and fostering innovation in the green sector.
reforms.source_quote: The government replaced the FIT in January 2012 with a Renewable Portfolio Standard (RPS), which obliges electricity companies to produce or buy a specified share of their electricity from renewable sources.

reforms.description: The government plans to expand its green R&D budget from 2 trillion won in 2009 to 3.5 trillion won by 2013, focusing on 27 core green technologies to enhance innovation and competitiveness in the green sector.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Increasing public funding for green R&D is expected to stimulate innovation in key technologies, thereby enhancing Korea's competitiveness in the global green economy.
reforms.status_evidence: The government will expand its green R&D from 2 trillion won in 2009 to 3.5 trillion won by 2013.
reforms.importance_rationale: This significant increase in funding for green R&D reflects a strategic commitment to fostering innovation in critical areas of the green economy, which is essential for long-term sustainable growth.
reforms.source_quote: According to the Five-Year Plan, the government will expand its green R&D from 2 trillion won in 2009 to 3.5 trillion won by 2013.

reforms.description: The government plans to raise its share of public investment in basic research to 35% by 2012, up from 20% in 2009, to enhance social returns and spillover effects from research.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Increasing public investment in basic research is expected to yield high social returns and foster innovation, which can drive economic growth.
reforms.status_evidence: The government should follow through on its plan to raise its share to 35% in 2012.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform represents a significant shift in public funding priorities towards basic research, which is crucial for long-term innovation and economic development.
reforms.source_quote: The government should follow through on its plan to raise its share to 35% in 2012, given that such research has a public good character and is unlikely to be undertaken by private firms.

reforms.description: The government introduced mandatory standards for vehicle GHG emissions and average fuel efficiency in 2012, aiming to enhance energy efficiency and prepare for carbon pricing.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Implementing stringent vehicle emission standards is expected to drive innovation in automotive technologies and improve energy efficiency, contributing to sustainable growth.
reforms.status_evidence: The regulation will initially be applied to 30% of all sales and expanded to 100% by 2015.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform is a significant regulatory change aimed at improving vehicle efficiency, which can stimulate innovation in the automotive sector.
reforms.source_quote: The government introduced mandatory standards for vehicle GHG emissions and average fuel efficiency in 2012, at a level similar to that in the United States.

reforms.description: The government aims to increase the share of green R&D in total government spending from 16% in 2009 to 20% by 2013, while also raising the share of basic research from 15% to 35% between 2008 and 2012, to attract private investment in green technologies.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Increased funding for green R&D is expected to stimulate innovation and attract private investment, contributing to sustainable economic growth.
reforms.status_evidence: The government has set specific targets for increasing green R&D funding.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform represents a significant shift in funding priorities towards green technologies, which is crucial for achieving long-term sustainability and economic growth.
reforms.source_quote: Green R&D is to rise from 16% of the government’s total R&D spending in 2009 to 20% by 2013.

reforms.description: The government plans to improve the overall innovation framework by increasing spending on basic research and enhancing collaboration between research institutes, universities, and industry.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Enhancing collaboration is expected to reduce the mismatch between human resources and research spending, thereby fostering innovation and economic growth.
reforms.status_evidence: The government has proposed to improve the innovation framework.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform is significant as it aims to enhance the innovation ecosystem, although it is part of a broader strategy rather than a standalone major reform.
reforms.source_quote: Improve the overall innovation framework by spending more on basic research, closely linking government research institutes, universities and industry.

reforms.description: The government announced the 'Comprehensive Non-regular Workers Initiative' to address dualism in the labor market, focusing on strengthening the social safety net and enhancing equal treatment for non-regular workers.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: By improving the conditions and training opportunities for non-regular workers, the initiative aims to enhance their employability and stability, contributing to overall economic productivity.
reforms.status_evidence: The government announced in September 2011 the 'Comprehensive Non-regular Workers Initiative'.
reforms.importance_rationale: The initiative represents a significant effort to address labor market dualism and improve conditions for a large segment of the workforce, which is crucial for social cohesion and economic stability.
reforms.source_quote: The government announced in September 2011 the 'Comprehensive Non-regular Workers Initiative' to address dualism, primarily by strengthening the social safety net and enhancing equal treatment for non-regular workers.

reforms.description: The government proposed cutting university tuition fees by 30% by 2014, with an immediate reduction of 15% in 2012 through government spending and increased university grants.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Reducing tuition fees can increase access to higher education, thereby enhancing the skill level of the workforce and supporting long-term economic growth.
reforms.status_evidence: The government announced a reduction in tuition fees as part of its education reform agenda.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform addresses a critical issue in higher education affordability, impacting a large segment of the population and aiming to improve educational equity and access.
reforms.source_quote: In June 2011, the ruling party proposed cutting tuition fees by 30% by 2014.
